title: fraxinus anomala var. lowellii
intro: Fraxinus anomala var. lowellii, also known as the Green Ash or Lowell's Ash, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and slender branches. The bark and leaves of the Green Ash tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Fraxinus anomala var. lowellii include relief from fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as support for the urinary system and digestive system. Fraxinus anomala var. lowellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
